violations.text: Establishment is lacking an allergy statement on their menu. An allergen statement shall be posted on menus and/or shall be made visible to consumers, alerting them to notify staff of any food allergies. i.e. "Before placing your order, please inform your server if a person in your party has a food allergy." or "Please inform your server of any food allergies." New Violation. Correct By: 09/08/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer and Univex mixer) were observed with food debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer cleaning guidance provided. New Violation. Correct By: 09/08/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of residue. Observed residue dripping directly in ice. PIC voluntarily dispose of ice and clean ice machine at time of inspection. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/08/2025

All foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024
Some kitchen floor tiles were missing grout in the (3-bay sink) area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

The presence of pests is not controlled as evidenced by an accumulation of rodent droppings near mop sink and front service line and dead or trapped roaches observed in front service area and back prep area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of table/countertops and areas under equipment were observed with food and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024

The following areas of the physical facilities were observed in poor repair: 1) Grout missing throughout the kitchen floor 2) Floor near the prep sink has cracked or missing tiles allowing for area of pooling water and debris that may act as a point of pest harborage Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024

Clutter (maintenance tools, etc.) was observed above walk-in cooler. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2024

The presence of rodents and roaches is not controlled as evidenced by an accumulation of rodent droppings, a dead mouse, and live and dead roach activity observed. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

The following non-food contact surfaces were observed with an accumulation of debris: 1) Bottom area of prep tables had food debris and small amount of rodent droppings 2) Pipes and equipment on the cook line had a large accumulation of grease, soil residue, and other debris Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2024

The following areas of the physical facilities were observed in poor repair and could contribute to pest harborage and activity: 1) Grout missing throughout kitchen floor 2) Several holes observed (near grease trap, near mop sink, and in upper storage areas) 3) Cracks and gaps observed throughout tiles that meet in the floor to wall junctures New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024

A large amount of clutter was observed above the walk-in cooler and second floor storage area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2024
A pan was observed in the back hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

Raw animal foods (such as chicken and shrimp) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as vegetables and cooked chicken) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC voluntarily disposed of ice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

All foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chicken and pork were observed cooling in tightly wrapped containers. PIC instructed to remove plastic wrap to allow for proper cooling. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

The presence of roaches and rodents is not controlled as evidenced by live and dead roaches as well as a large accumulation of rodent droppings observed throughout the establishment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of bottom shelving/counter areas had an accumulation of debris and rodent droppings. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

The food prep sink is lacking an adequate air gap. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024
The floor throughout the prep and ware-washing areas were in poor repair (grout missing). New Violation. Correct By: 08/30/2024

A large amount of clutter was observed above the walk-in cooler and sercond floor storage area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2024

All foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/15/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Metal containers of chicken were observed wrapped in plastic wrap. Plastic wrap was removed from containers. Proper cooling methods reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/15/2023

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. A bottle of soap was brought over to the hand-washing sink. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2023

Raw animal foods (eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (seafood) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Handout was provided to PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2023

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. Equipment was placed in 3-bay sink. Proper sanitizing procedures reviewed with PIC. Food contact surfaces of the ice machine was observed with an accumulation of bio-film. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2023

All foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. PIC will start using date dots for datemarking. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2023
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2023
The reach-in freezer door gasket was observed in poor repair. The hot water handles at both hand sinks were in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023
The grout in the ware washing area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023
